title: Differences in logging between Idaho and Michigan
description: Difference between logging in Idaho and Michigan's Upper Peninsula. Better conditions in Idaho camps than Michigan's.

title: What the IWW accomplished; the democracy of the IWW
description: The IWW's accomplished wonders with direct action. The strike in 1923 was for the prisoners in jail; the 1936 strike for better conditions, which were improved after they broke the strike. Democracy of IWW made it much better than the CIO. IWW's strike for better conditions, not money. They were revolutionary and are dead.

title: Lumberjacks are single; the work was all done by hand; lumberjacks were mostly older and came from the east
description: People have changed. Lumberjacks were single, footloose, and had the world to gain. Gyppo system was supported by many to make money, and was opposed by the IWW. The work was all by hand and hard. Older men learned shortcuts. Lumberjacks were mostly older and came from the east.

title: His father had a sawmill in Quebec, Canada
description: His father had a sawmill in Quebec, Canada.

title: Fights among lumberjacks; Aberdeen was roughest town on the coast
description: Fights among lumberjacks. Aberdeen was the roughest town on the coast.

title: Staying on the job; working with good foreman
description: Staying with the job. The foreman rarely checked on gyppos, or bothered working men on the job. Most foremen worked for the company. Malker Anderson was a very good foreman.

title: Consolidation of the Potlatch Lumber company forests; taking horse teams to Headquarters; the ground around Bovill
description: Consolidation of Potlatch Forests. The Potlatch Lumber Company woods operation had better conditions than the Clearwater; quality of Potlatch horses. The home meadow at Bovill. Taking horse teams from Elk River to Headquarters. The ground around Bovill was almost all accessible to railroad.

title: The death of Art Henderson; early logging camps on the Clearwater; cutting the road on the river
description: Death of Art Henderson. Early logging and camps on the Clearwater, and foremen from Montana. Cutting the road on the river.

title: First Clearwater river log drives; construction of early wanigans; Gust Piper took the first drives; Silent Joe trapped on the North Fork
description: First Clearwater River log drives. Construction of early wanigans - cedar timbers, vine maple binders. Gust Piper was a log "pirate" who took rafts down to the Snake; he took the first drives because he knew the whole river. Silent Joe trapped on the North Fork in different cabins and rarely spoke.

title: Navigating the wanigans; finding places to land
description: Navigating the wanigans. Swinging athwart the current. Need to keep rafts together. Finding places to land; setting snublines.

title: A raft that missed the landing and the foreman was let go
description: A raft that missed the landing went thirty miles to Big Island and the foreman was canned.

title: Discussion about rear and center crews on the drives; lining the batteaus upstream
description: Rear crews and center crews on the river drives. Using powder. Lining the batteaus upstream.

title: Working in the riving; the drowning of three men near Lenore
description: Working in the river. Drowning of three men when their boat swamped near Lenore. Fitting searched for his brother's body.

title: Taking up collections by the men in camps - a day's wages
description: Taking up collections by the men in camps - a day's wages.

title: Discussion of Pierce, Idaho and Clarkia; how the Pierce hotel started
description: Pierce, a lumberjack town with everything. Tent living in town. Pierce Hotel started as a bootleg shack. Clarkia, a wide open town.

title: Incline on Marble Creek; Working for Wood 'Em Up George
description: Incline on Marble Creek - 57% grade. Working for Wood 'Em Up George - he lost money with race horses, he liked to fire men.

title: Moving from camp to camp; Oscar Sturgill's camp; death of a superintendent
description: Going from camp to camp. Getting jobs. Oscar Sturgill's camp. Tramp lumberjacks and family men. Death of a superintendent in a wind storm.
